item.title: Argentina’s energy economy booms amid Iran shock
item.description: High oil and gas export revenues could be an unexpected windfall for the South American nation

item.title: BYD profits drop by more than half as Chinese sales slow
item.description: Phasing out of electric vehicle subsidies has driven slump in domestic sales growth following years-long boom

item.title: First ‘autocallable’ ETF launched in Europe
item.description: Coupon-paying fund aimed at retail investors

item.title: Grant Thornton US to buy Australian sister firm as part of global spree
item.description: Private equity-backed accounting group vies with UK business to create international network

item.title: ‘Half-amused, half-ashamed’: the employees facing phone bans at work
item.description: Some companies are asking staff to lock devices in pouches to remove distractions and protect data

item.title: Novartis sales fall as blockbuster patents expire
item.description: Swiss pharma group blames competition from generic medicines for 12% drop in first-quarter profit

item.title: Barclays overcomes £200mn credit hit to boost profits
item.description: Fee income in investment banking business helps drive pre-tax profits 3% higher to £2.8bn in first quarter

item.title: BP profits hit highest since 2023 as Iran war delivers windfall
item.description: Oil major’s quarterly profit beats analysts’ expectations on back of ‘exceptional’ trading performance

item.title: Europe’s pharma industry risks lagging behind US and China, says German drugmaker
item.description: Boehringer Ingelheim says pricing regime hinders access to treatments as group touts liver benefits from weight-loss drug

item.title: Wall Street is getting twitchy on oil prices
item.description: Plus, dollar dreams and reality

item.title: FirstFT: Germany’s Merz warns US ‘being humiliated’ by Iran
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: MPs vote on probe into Starmer, and Orbán allies dwindle

item.title: Two Spirit Airlines creditor groups back Trump bailout plan
item.description: Bondholder group remains divided over $500mn on offer from White House that would rescue bankrupt budget carrier

item.title: World’s biggest battery maker to raise $5bn in ‘opportunistic’ share placement
item.description: China’s CATL rides surge in foreign investor interest in renewable energy stocks since outbreak of war in Iran

item.title: The airline merger paradox
item.description: The industry’s recent challenges pose a dilemma for US antitrust enforcers

item.title: Abu Dhabi state energy group to invest ‘tens of billions’ in US push
item.description: Adnoc accelerates effort to diversify into US natural gas as Iran war rattles Middle Eastern energy industry

item.title: Milken family office alumni raise $4bn to target private capital upheaval
item.description: Silver Rock Capital Partners draws in biggest credit fund to date

item.title: Italy clashes with Switzerland over medical bills from nightclub fire
item.description: Families have complained after receiving invoices for their children’s emergency treatment

item.title: European airlines seize on jet fuel crisis to lobby against passenger perks
item.description: Proposals such as extra free baggage allowance would make it harder to cope with rising costs, industry says

item.title: Mexico City seeks new rent controls as opposition to gentrification grows
item.description: Shortage of affordable homes and protests over US immigrants prompt policy response

item.title: Heathrow’s third runway at risk if Reeves is removed after May elections
item.description: UK chancellor has been driving force behind latest attempt to expand London hub airport

item.title: Middle East war boosts ship-tracking app as millions follow maritime traffic
item.description: Kpler reports surging demand from governments, traders and media watching ships and energy flows

item.title: Rivian is bringing software-as-a-service to the road
item.description: Electric-vehicle maker wants to license its code for other carmakers

item.title: Wall Street dealers boost Treasury holdings to highest level since 2007
item.description: Trump administration’s push to cut regulations prompts big banks to facilitate more trading in government debt

item.title: Energy tax cuts spread to 39 economies as fuel prices jump
item.description: Reductions in energy levies come despite IMF warning against ‘untargeted’ support

item.title: A troubling plan to revive the frozen US property market
item.description: Buyers should beware the real estate dark pools

item.title: Why investors are chasing US biotechs with Chinese characteristics
item.description: Drugs developed in Asian nation are increasingly being used to seed US start-ups

item.title: After conquering nickel, Indonesia sets its sights on aluminium
item.description: Analysts fear Chinese-funded build-up of smelters will lead to oversupply

item.title: The great American data centre divide
item.description: Many rural communities are viscerally opposed to AI infrastructure, putting them at odds with the White House

item.title: London landlord sues John Lewis in click-and-collect dispute
item.description: Owner of Brent Cross shopping centre argues that 47-year-old lease entitles it to a cut of John Lewis’s online sales

item.title: Private equity deals where the seller is also the buyer
item.description: Investors question if some institutions rubber-stamp deals that could benefit other businesses
